mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 349436M as of 2004-12-01 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, monetary tightening constrains equity valuations by raising the risk-free discount rate and reducing corporate profit margins. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of 2004-12-01 is below tightening threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.0% sustained, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60d returns is positive and > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice equity risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of 2004-12-01 shows stable labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of instances following UNRATE jumps > 0.3pp, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 5d returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and supply-side strength. Sustained month-over-month INDPRO growth of > 0.5% signals robust production capacity and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.813 as of 2004-12-01 reflects strong industrial activity
regime_hint: expansion phase with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances when INDPRO growth > 0.5%, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. When both series show positive 3-month momentum simultaneously, the interaction amplifies confidence in sustained earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive 3-month momentum
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1771K and RSAFS at 349436M as of 2004-12-01 both elevated
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in < 60% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS show positive 3-month momentum, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) interact to signal labor market tightness. When PAYEMS grows > 150K month-over-month while UNRATE remains below 5.5%, the combination indicates strong wage pressure and robust corporate hiring, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132266K and UNRATE at 5.4% as of 2004-12-01
regime_hint: tight labor market in expansion phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in < 58% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term has negative coefficient in rolling regressions (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%), the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy, keeping FEDFUNDS below 3.0%. This monetary accommodation compresses equity risk premiums and supports valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 191.2 and FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of 2004-12-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in < 62% of instances when both conditions hold, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and 20d returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly provides the broadest signal of economic demand strength. When annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, equity earnings expectations rise, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10891.0B (2017 dollars) as of 2004-07-01 reflects strong economic expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in < 60% of instances when GDPC1 annualized growth > 3.0%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60d returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
